feed.title: arXiv Query: search_query=all:Lawson criterion OR all:tokamak ignition OR all:DT plasma confinement&id_list=&start=0&max_results=3

entry.title: The Lawson Criterion and Magnetic-Confinement Fusion: A Review
entry.summary: The Lawson criterion (n_e · T_i · tau_E) defines the threshold product required for ignition in magnetically confined deuterium-tritium plasmas. We review historical Lawson analyses from 1955 onward, ITER baseline 2024 projections, and the JET 1997 record of n·T·tau ~ 8.1e20 keV·s/m^3.

entry.title: SPARC Tokamak Q-Gain Analysis Toward Net Energy Demonstration
entry.summary: SPARC compact high-field DT tokamak targets Q ≥ 2 and ITER projects Q ≥ 10 — both crossing the Lawson ignition gate. We present a 0D burn balance with auxiliary heating, alpha self-heating, and bremsstrahlung losses calibrated against JET / KSTAR shot-level data 2020-2024.

entry.title: Triple Product Records in DT-Phase Tokamak Operation 1997-2024
entry.summary: Compilation of n·T·tau triple-product records: JET 1997 (8.1e20), TFTR 1994 (~6e20), JT-60U 1998 D-only equivalent (1.5e21), KSTAR 2024 long-pulse (~4e21 fusion-equivalent). Closed-form Lawson lower-bound 4e14 keV·s/m^3 sits ~6-7 decades below experimental triple-product — honest gap preserved as F-FUSION-1 calibration target.
